skill.name: notion-template-business
skill.description: Guides creators through designing, pricing, launching, marketing, and supporting Notion templates as digital products.
skill.summary: Build and sell Notion templates with clear positioning, pricing, launch, and support plans.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are Markdown formatting and business guidance text, with no executable scripts, no network calls, and no prompt injection attempts. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from community-source provenance.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences and inline skill references inside SKILL.md. They do not instruct the model to run shell commands, Ruby code, or external programs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are Markdown fences or inline names, and no executable command syntax is present. The surrounding content describes Notion template business strategy.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are keyword collisions
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain business terms such as design, pricing, SEO, and template descriptions. No cryptographic algorithm, host discovery, or network scanning behavior appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found natural language marketing and product guidance only. There is no code path, command, or operational instruction for cryptography or reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Network library finding is normal support vocabulary
low_findings.description: The network finding maps to the phrase refund requests. This is customer support guidance and not a Python HTTP library import or outbound request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is plain English about support issues. No import statement, URL, request call, or data transfer instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Creators often struggle to turn useful Notion templates into paid products. This skill helps plan the template, pricing, sales channels, launch content, and support system.
actual_capabilities: Define a paid Notion template concept around a specific customer problem.
Create pricing tiers, bundles, and value-based pricing recommendations.
Compare sales channels such as Gumroad, Lemon Squeezy, Notion Marketplace, and a direct website.
Plan launch assets, product pages, documentation, and support workflows.
Identify common business risks such as weak positioning, poor support, and marketplace dependence.
limitations: It does not create or publish pages inside a Notion workspace by itself.
It does not guarantee sales, marketplace approval, or payment processor acceptance.
It does not replace legal, tax, or financial advice for a digital product business.
It relies on user-provided audience, niche, and product details for specific recommendations.

use_cases.title: Validate a template idea
use_cases.description: Assess the target buyer, problem, feature set, and reason someone would pay for the template.

use_cases.title: Package and price a launch
use_cases.description: Turn one template into clear tiers, bundles, bonuses, and launch messaging for a first release.

use_cases.title: Improve an existing template store
use_cases.description: Review positioning, sales channels, documentation, support load, and update strategy for stronger operations.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a template niche
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a Notion template niche. My audience is [audience], their problem is [problem], and my experience is [experience].

prompt_templates.title: Design the template package
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a paid Notion template package for [buyer type]. Include the main dashboard, core pages, examples, documentation, and bonuses.

prompt_templates.title: Create pricing and bundles
prompt_templates.prompt: Create pricing tiers for my Notion template about [topic]. Include basic, pro, and premium offers with clear value differences.

prompt_templates.title: Audit my sales system
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my Notion template business. Review my niche, offer, pricing, sales channels, landing page, support process, and update plan.

output_examples.input: I want to sell a finance tracker for freelancers.
output: Position the template around cash flow clarity for independent workers.
Include income tracking, expense categories, tax estimates, invoice status, and monthly review pages.
Offer a basic tracker, a pro version with guides, and a premium bundle with example workflows.

output_examples.input: My template is useful, but nobody buys it.
output: Review whether the sales page names a specific buyer and urgent problem.
Add screenshots, a short walkthrough, proof examples, and a free lead magnet.
Test one direct channel and one marketplace channel before changing the product.

output_examples.input: I need a support plan for paid customers.
output: Create a getting started guide, feature walkthrough, FAQ, and update notes page.
Reserve direct email or chat support for higher tiers.
Track repeated questions and turn them into documentation updates.

best_practices: Start with a clear buyer, painful problem, and measurable value before designing pages.
Use examples, documentation, and screenshots to reduce purchase hesitation and support requests.
Diversify sales channels after the first product proves demand.
anti_patterns: Do not build a generic template without a defined audience or buying reason.
Do not set prices only by copying other creators without considering customer value.
Do not launch paid templates without support documentation and an update plan.

faq.question: Can this skill build the Notion template for me?
faq.answer: It can plan the structure, pages, documentation, and launch assets. It does not directly edit your Notion workspace.

faq.question: Does it cover pricing strategy?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests pricing tiers, bundles, value framing, and channel-specific pricing considerations.

faq.question: Can it help with Gumroad or Lemon Squeezy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline product setup, offer structure, descriptions, launch assets, and support flows for these platforms.

faq.question: Is it useful for free templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It can position a free template as a lead magnet, upsell path, or trust-building asset.

faq.question: Does it guarantee marketplace approval?
faq.answer: No. It can improve quality and positioning, but marketplace approval depends on each platform review process.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it if you want to sell Notion templates or improve an existing template product business.
